exemplar.title: How does the concentration of Al(OH)3 antacid solution (mol/dm3) affect the digestion of gelatinby pepsin, as measured through the absorbance (nm) of the biuret-gelatin solution using a colorimeter (565 nm)?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the concentration of Al(OH)3 antacid solution (mol/dm3) affect the digestion of gelatinby pepsin, as measured through the absorbance (nm) of the biuret-gelatin solution using a colorimeter (565 nm)?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Background literature is effectively cited, linking molecular pH effects to pepsin activity for strong biochemical context.
Step-by-step procedure gives exact reagent masses, volumes, incubation times and calibration steps, enabling reproducibility.
Independent variable justification links antacid concentrations directly to physiological pH changes, demonstrating clear methodological consideration.
weaknesses: Materials list omits calibration steps for volumetric pipettes, risking slight reproducibility ambiguity.
Control-variable table headings are incomplete and a justification column cuts off, reducing clarity.
Sequence confusion between 37 Â°C water bath and 70 Â°C hot plate needs clarification to avoid procedural ambiguity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs research design demonstrates a clearly defined context (band 5), thorough methodological considerations (band 5) and an almost flawless reproducible procedure (band 6). Minor omissions in calibration detail and table formatting prevent a full top-band score. A holistic band 5 (out of 6) fairly reflects these strengths and areas for improvement.

strengths: Raw and processed data are tabulated with correct units, uncertainties and clear headings, with 14 consistent trials.
Advanced statistical analyses (Pearson r, RÂ², ANOVA, Tukey post-hoc and polynomial regression) are executed and applied accurately.
Data-processing rationale is clearly described, distinguishing raw versus processed steps and reflecting appropriate uncertainty consideration.
weaknesses: Graph axis label uses ânmâ instead of âAUâ, requiring correction.
The Pearson formula presentation omits a square root in the denominator for the y-term and should be adjusted.
Some procedural uncertainties remain high (~35%), suggesting refinement in mixing and timing methods is needed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication and processing of data are both clear and precise (band 6), consideration of uncertainties is appropriate (band 6) and data processing is carried out accurately (band 6). Although minor presentation issues exist, the sophistication and accuracy of the analyses justify a full band 6.

strengths: Conclusion directly answers the research question and references the polynomial model, F-value and RÂ² for consistency.
Insightful comparison to MichaelisâMenten kinetics with correctly cited literature, providing strong scientific context.
Clinical implications are clearly drawn, linking pepsin inhibition to nutritional risks, enhancing relevance.
weaknesses: The final paragraph breaks mid-sentence, disrupting the flow and completeness of the argument.
Minor logical leap when equating procedural uncertainty with overall reliability needs tighter justification.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is fully consistent with the analysis (band 6) and justified through relevant comparison to accepted scientific context (band 6). Despite small coherence issues, the depth of interpretation and strong contextualization merit the top band.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (e.g., Biuret assay specificity, limited concentration range) are clearly described.
Realistic improvements (alternative assays, wider concentration series, in-situ pH monitoring) are relevant to identified limitations.
Impact of each improvement on reducing error or revealing saturation kinetics is explained coherently.
weaknesses: Depth of explanation on the relative impact of equipment-related weaknesses could be enhanced further.
Omission of direct pH measurements in the evaluation section leaves one improvement under-justified.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation describes specific methodological weaknesses and proposes realistic improvements linked to each limitation, fitting band 5. Though the explanations are coherent, they lack the depth required for a full band 6, justifying a score of 5.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion directly answers the research question, references the polynomial model, F-value and RÂ², and links absorbance changes to reduced digestion. Minor logical leaps occur when equating procedural uncertainty with reliability, so justification is sound but not flawlessly tight.

Raw data are tabulated with correct units/uncertainties and processed data clearly distinguished. Graphs include titles, axes, units and error bars. Minor imprecision exists (graph units ânmâ instead of âAUâ in title), so the work is clear and mostly preciseâsecure band-5.

Averages, SD, Pearson r, RÂ², ANOVA, Tukey post-hoc and regression modelling are executed accurately and used to refine the trend as polynomial. Calculations are shown and values internally consistent with raw data. Processing is both appropriate and accurate, meriting full marks.

Specific weaknessesâBiuret assay specificity, limited concentration range, lack of pH measurementâare described with discussion of how each skews absorbance accuracy or masks saturation kinetics. Impact is explained but depth could improve for equipment choice, thus a firm band-5.

The student explicitly compares findings to Michaelis-Menten kinetics and published pH-activity curves, explaining agreement and divergence. Literature is correctly cited and integrated into the argument, fulfilling the descriptor for a fully justified comparison.

The student frames the question within a clearly defined biochemical context (pepsin activity, optimal pH, antacid mode of action). Background literature is cited and directly linked to the research question, specifying why antacid concentrations chosen are physiologically relevant. While highly specific, the link to real-life gastrointestinal conditions could be tightened (e.g. stomach volume assumptions), so a secure, but not flawless, level-5 description is most fitting.

Instrumental uncertainty, total percent uncertainty, and procedural uncertainty are calculated and displayed. Error bars reflect SD, not combined uncertainty, but the student recognises multiple uncertainty sources and comments on their magnitude. Apart from a few formula presentation slips, consideration is appropriate, placing the work high in the band but not exemplary.

Proposed improvements (alternative assays, wider concentration series, in-situ pH monitoring) are realistic and clearly linked to the stated weaknesses, with explanations of how each would reduce error or reveal enzyme saturation. The reasoning is coherent though not extensively prioritised, matching band-5 expectations.

Variables, controls, instrumental tolerances and statistical tests are discussed, demonstrating an understanding of how sufficient data will be gathered (14 trials, percent and procedural uncertainties, ANOVA to confirm trend type). Relative impact on data quality is touched on in evaluation rather than here, so the explanations, although strong, are not consistently integrated throughout, warranting solid band-5 performance.

The step-by-step procedure gives exact reagent masses, volumes, incubation times, temperatures, vortex speeds and colourimeter settings. Separate pipettes are labelled to avoid cross-contamination, and calibration steps are included. Another investigator could replicate the work with minimal ambiguity, justifying the top of the band.

annotations.comment: The research question is well defined but could further integrate assumptions such as gastric volume to strengthen physiological relevance.
Background literature is effectively cited and concisely links molecular pH effects to pepsin activity, giving the study strong biochemical context.
Figure 1 is informative but would benefit from explicit reference in the methodology to clarify how the biuret complex informs absorbance measurements.
The independent variable justification clearly links antacid concentrations to physiological pH changes, demonstrating strong methodological consideration.
Absorbance is reported in ânmâ rather than arbitrary units (AU), which could mislead interpretationâcorrect axis labels to AU.
The controlâvariable table provides rationale but the justification column cuts off midâexplanation; expand to fully articulate control choices.
Controlâvariable table lacks descriptive column headings, reducing clarity on variable categorization; ensure headings are explicit.
The temperature control is well justified with reference to enzyme kinetics and maintained at 37 Â°C, showing strong methodological planning.
The hypothesis section offers detailed mechanistic reasoning tying pH shifts to enzyme protonation states, providing exceptional conceptual clarity.
Materials list is comprehensive but omits how volumetric pipettes are calibrated; include calibration steps to ensure reproducibility.
Step 10 introduces a hot plate at 70 Â°C despite earlier use of a 37 Â°C water bath; clarify its purpose and sequence to avoid procedural confusion.
The safety section thoroughly addresses chemical hazards and disposal protocols, demonstrating strong ethical and environmental consideration.
Risk assessment could expand on in vitro limitations versus in vivo relevance to deepen ethical discussion of model validity.
The antacid concentration calculation is clearly shown with correct unit conversions, facilitating reproducibility.
Procedural uncertainty ranges up to 35 %, overshadowing low apparatus uncertainty; refine mixing and timing methods to reduce variability.
Qualitative data table is detailed but its title is truncated and misformatted; correct the heading for clear presentation.
Is an illustrative photograph included to support qualitative observations and improve reader interpretation?
Raw data table includes 14 trials with consistent significant figures and units, indicating thorough data collection.
Processed data table shows procedural uncertainties up to 35 %, suggesting unsatisfactory precision despite accurate SD calculation.
The dataâprocessing rationale is clearly described, distinguishing raw versus processed results and analytical steps.
The Pearson formula omits the square root in the denominator for y-term summationâcorrect this to accurately compute r.
Post-hoc Tukeyâs test table uses âinsignificantâ inconsistently; explicitly state p-value thresholds (e.g., p > 0.05) to clarify criteria.
Interpretation of overlapping error bars and subsequent ANOVA correctly identifies nonlinearity, showing strong consideration of uncertainty.
The concluding paragraph breaks mid-sentence, disrupting coherence; ensure the full argument is presented without truncation.
Comparison to MichaelisâMenten kinetics is insightful and justified through published curves, fulfilling scientific context expectations.
Reiteration of peptide-bond mechanism in conclusion repeats earlier sections; condense to maintain focus on interpretation.
The clinical implication for antacid users is drawn clearly, linking pepsin inhibition to nutritional risks, enhancing conclusion relevance.
Limitation notes the Biuret assay but omits direct pH monitoring; adding pH measurements would strengthen the evaluation.
The evaluation acknowledges limited concentration range impacting kinetic interpretation, demonstrating specific methodological critique.
Suggest including a pH probe to verify solution pH before and after digestion, directly linking pepsin activity to measured acidity.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The research question is stated without context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting data relevant to the research question are stated.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data lacks the detail to allow for the investigation to be reproduced.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced with few ambiguities or omissions.
The research question is outlined within a broad context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are described.
The research question is described within a specific and appropriate context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are explained.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The recording and processing of the data is communicated but is neither clear nor precise.
Some processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with major omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The recording and processing of data shows limited evidence of the consideration of uncertainties.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Communication of data recording and processing

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The conclusion makes superficial comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is stated that is relevant to the research question but is not supported by the analysis presented.
A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
Realistic improvements to the investigation are stated.
The report states generic methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.
The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
The report explains the relative impact of specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
